desc: Placencia is Belize's laid-back Caribbean peninsula for reef access, cayes, and inland jungle add-ons, with the biggest planning decision being dry-season convenience versus rainy-season value.
hls: Top base for boat trips to Silk Caye, Laughing Bird Caye, and barrier-reef snorkel/dives.
Placencia Village, Seine Bight, and Maya Beach offer different vibes from walkable to resort-style.
Dry season (roughly Feb–Apr) usually brings the clearest beach conditions and easiest logistics.
Rainier months can still work well for slower stays, lower rates, and greener inland excursions.
Good split-stay candidate with Cockscomb Basin, Hopkins, or San Ignacio for jungle contrast.
Strong seafood-focused dining with a relaxed barefoot-evening atmosphere.

todo.name: Barrier reef snorkel or dive day
todo.desc: Book a full-day reef trip (often with cayes stop) and prioritize calm-weather windows for best visibility.

todo.name: Placencia Sidewalk + village food loop
todo.desc: Walk the village lanes and beach stretch, stopping at small cafés and seafood grills.

todo.name: Cockscomb Basin wildlife day
todo.desc: Head inland for river tubing, trails, and birdwatching in Belize's protected rainforest zone.

todo.name: Sailing/sunset cruise
todo.desc: Use a half-day lagoon or coastal sail for sunset light and quieter peninsula views.

todo.name: Maya Beach chill day
todo.desc: Keep one low-structure beach day for paddleboarding, swimming, and recovery between tours.

todo.name: Garifuna culture add-on
todo.desc: Pair with a Hopkins visit for drumming, food, and Garifuna heritage experiences.

wifi.note: Solid in higher-end resorts and cowork-friendly cafés; slower or less stable in budget stays and stormy weather.

prac.fltNote: No regular UK nonstop to Placencia; typical routing is UK → Belize City (BZE) via US/Central America, then domestic flight to Placencia (PLJ) or 3–4 hour road transfer. Return fares are often ~£620–£980 off-peak, ~£980–£1,300 in shoulder demand, and ~£1,300+ over Christmas/New Year and Easter periods.

airports.name: Philip S. W. Goldson International Airport (Belize City)

prac.currency: Belize Dollar (BZD, pegged to USD at 2:1); USD is widely accepted, but change may return in BZD.
prac.lang: English is official; Belizean Kriol and Spanish are common in daily interactions.
prac.visa: Check Belize entry requirements and any transit documentation needed for your connection routing.
alerts: Hurricane season (Jun–Nov) can disrupt marine outings and domestic flight schedules.
Seaweed (sargassum) levels vary by period and wind pattern—check recent local reports if beach quality is a priority.
ATMs are limited on the peninsula versus Belize City, so keep a cash buffer.

sweet: February to April is usually the easiest all-round window for reef visibility and beach weather; June and November can offer better prices with manageable weather flexibility.
